result.summary: Zuplo exposes a documented Developer API at `https://dev.zuplo.com`, an npm-installed `zuplo` CLI plus public `create-zuplo-api` scaffolder, and tenant-defined MCP endpoints on deployed gateways using OAuth-protected bearer access.
result.description: Zuplo is a managed API gateway platform for building, securing, deploying, and observing APIs. It also supports MCP server and MCP gateway patterns so teams can expose APIs and AI tools through the same gateway infrastructure.

zuplo_oauth.label: Zuplo account OAuth login / access token
zuplo_oauth.setup: Sign in to the [Zuplo Portal](https://portal.zuplo.com/). For CLI use, run `zuplo login` to authenticate with your Zuplo account via OAuth. The docs indicate the CLI authenticates with your Zuplo account via OAuth, and the API reference is served from Zuplo’s authenticated developer docs rather than a separately documented self-service API key flow.

surfaces.name: Zuplo MCP Gateway

requiredHeaders.name: Accept
requiredHeaders.description: Required on requests to MCP routes per the Streamable HTTP transport spec.

surfaces.notes: Customer-defined MCP route paths are hosted on each deployed gateway. The reference documents OAuth authorization-server metadata at `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` and protected-resource metadata at `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/{routePath*}`, plus bearer access tokens issued by `/oauth/token` with scope `mcp:tools`.
